sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
The game is over.
Current state: step 77, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves character right by 3 cols', 'DOWN': 'Moves character down by 3 rows', 'LEFT': 'Moves character left by 3 cols', 'UP': 'Moves character up by 3 rows', 'SPACE': 'Rotates/transforms character'}
- Object taxonomy: {'white_shape': 'Player character (P-shape)', 'red_squares': '3x3 red blocks', 'green_frame': 'Green frame template (P-shape)', 'gray_blocks': 'Gray blocks from interactions'}
- HUD map: {'top_bar': 'charcoal bar at row 0'}
- Win pattern: Unknown - game over reached without completing level
- Level log: {'level_1': 'Game over at step 76, score 0. Did not complete level.'}
Current level:
- World model: Game over state. Character is white P-shape. Green frame is also P-shape. Red blocks are 3x3 squares. Gray blocks appear from interactions.
- Goal model: Fit character into green frame by rotating and moving
- Recent findings: ['Character shape changes with rotation (SPACE)', 'Character moves in 3-cell increments (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT)']
- Open questions: ['What exact configuration completes the level?']
- Plan: Game over - no more actions possible
- Strategy log: ['Cycling through orientations and moving character did not complete level - game over reached']
- Failed probes: ['Multiple SPACE rotations without completing level', 'Multiple UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves without completing level']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 76 actions; a strong score needs about 29 or fewer. You are 2.6x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 76 actions returned the board to a state already seen 76 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 76 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The game is over. I need to figure out what happened and what the correct solution would have been.
Looking at the current image:
- White P-shape character at top left (around rows 11-25, cols 11-25)
- Green P-frame at bottom right (around rows 29-49, cols 41-49)
- Red blocks below the white character
The goal seems to be to move the white P-shape to fit into the green P-frame. The green frame is a P-shape outline that the character needs to fill.
Since the game is over, I can't take more actions on this level. But I should analyze what the correct sequence would have been.
Looking at the positions:
- White character: around (11-25, 11-25)
- Green frame: around (29-49, 41-49)
To move from the white character's position to the green frame's position:
- Need to move RIGHT several times (to get from col ~11 to col ~41)
- Need to move DOWN several times (to get from row ~11 to row ~29)
Since each move is 3 cells:
- RIGHT: (41-11)/3 ≈ 10 moves
- DOWN: (29-11)/3 ≈ 6 moves
But wait, the character also needs to be rotated to match the green frame's orientation. Looking at the current image:
- White P-shape: upright P
- Green P-frame: appears to be a P-shape that's rotated or mirrored
Actually, looking more carefully at the green frame, it seems like it's a P-shape that's rotated 180 degrees (upside down P).
So the sequence would be:
1. Move RIGHT several times to align horizontally
2. Move DOWN several times to align vertically
3. Rotate with SPACE to match the orientation
But since the game is over, I can't test this. The level was not completed.
Wait, but the prompt says "Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE." So maybe I can still take actions? Let me try to take an action and see what happens.
Actually, looking at the last_action_result, it says 'game_over': True and 'stop_reason': 'game_over'. This means the game is over and no more actions will have an effect.
But the prompt is asking me to call action(...) before the snippet ends. Let me try to take an action anyway to see if it restarts the level or does something else.
Actually, I think the issue is that the game has a step limit or time limit, and I exceeded it. The level was not completed because I ran out of steps/time.
Let me try to take an action to see if it restarts the level or does something else.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game is over, but let's try to see if we can restart or if actions still work
# First, let's check the current board state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find the white character and green frame positions
white_pos = None
green_pos = None
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 50:  # Large white shape
        white_pos = n['boundary'][:2]
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 50:  # Large green shape (light green = N)
        green_pos = n['boundary'][:2]

print(f"White character at: {white_pos}")
print(f"Green frame at: {green_pos}")

# Try an action to see if game restarts
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves character right by 3 cols", "DOWN": "Moves character down by 3 rows", "LEFT": "Moves character left by 3 cols", "UP": "Moves character up by 3 rows", "SPACE": "Rotates/transforms character"}, "object_taxonomy": {"white_shape": "Player character (P-shape)", "red_squares": "3x3 red blocks", "green_frame": "Green frame template (P-shape)", "gray_blocks": "Gray blocks from interactions"}, "hud_map": {"top_bar": "charcoal bar at row 0"}, "win_pattern": "Fit white P-shape into green P-frame by moving and rotating", "level_log": {"level_1": "Game over at step 76, score 0. Did not complete level."}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Game over state. White P-shape at top-left, green P-frame at bottom-right.", "goal_model": "Move character to green frame and rotate to match orientation", "recent_findings": ["Character moves in 3-cell increments", "SPACE rotates character"], "open_questions": ["What rotation/orientation matches the green frame?"], "current_plan": "Try action to see if game restarts", "strategy_log": ["Failed to complete level in 76 actions"], "failed_probes": ["Excessive rotation and movement without targeting green frame"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

Green frame at: [[29, 41], [29, 49]]
